trade.summary: Brasile and Messico are Latin America's two largest economies with complementary trade structures. Brasile exports iron ore, crude oil, chemicals, and agricultural goods. Messico exports vehicles, electronics, and manufactured goods. An FTA has been discussed but not concluded.
disputes: Ongoing auto industry disputes over trade barriers

diplomatic.summary: Brasile and Messico are natural regional partners but also regional competitors for Latin American leadership. Relations have periodically been tense due to different foreign policy orientations. Under Lula and AMLO/Sheinbaum, both pursue non-alignment but with some policy differences.

military.summary: No military alliance. Minimal military cooperation. Both countries maintain non-aligned defense postures. Some information sharing on transnational crime.

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Brasileian companies (Embraer, WEG, Marfrig) have Mexican operations. Mexican conglomerates (Cemex, América Móvil) are active in Brasile. Growing cross-border M&A activity.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Both are significant oil producers with domestically-oriented energy sectors. Some ethanol trade discussions.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration in agriculture, climate change, and social sciences. CONACYT-CAPES programs support researcher exchanges. Joint research in biodiversity and health.
